answers.answer: D) The new endpoint's unpaginated query holds a connection for 30+ seconds (explaining p99), exhausting the pool (48/50), so OTHER endpoints time out waiting for a free connection. Reason: This explains ALL symptoms: (1) DB CPU only 15% - DB itself is NOT overloaded, just holding connections open; (2) 48/50 connections near max - connections are being held by long-running queries; (3) p99 = 30 seconds - matches connection acquire timeout; (4) 500 errors on ALL endpoints - connection pool exhaustion affects everything, not just the new endpoint. Option A is wrong (DB not overloaded). Option B treats symptom not cause. Option C is wrong (errors are on all endpoints, not just new one).

NEXT.JS VERCEL BUILD ERROR ANALYSIS: 1. WHAT "Invariant: Method expects to have requestAsyncStorage" MEANS: This error occurs when Next.js code tries to access request-specific data (like headers, cookies, or params) outside of a request context. The `requestAsyncStorage` is Next.js's internal mechanism for tracking the current request's async context. When this storage is missing, it means the code is running at build time or in a context where no request is available. 2. MOST LIKELY CAUSE FOR EACH FAILING PATH: /api/webhook/stripe: - Likely using `headers()`, `cookies()`, or `useSearchParams()` at the module level or in a function that runs at build time - Webhook handlers should be dynamic (runtime) but may be incorrectly configured as static /(auth)/login/page: - Likely using server-side auth functions that expect request context at build time - May be calling auth functions in component body without proper async/request boundaries 3. SPECIFIC CODE PATTERNS CAUSING THIS: /api/webhook/stripe: ```typescript // WRONG - at module level const headersList = headers(); // Called at build time! export async function POST(req) { ... } ``` /(auth)/login/page: ```typescript // WRONG - calling auth at build time const session = await getSession(); // No request context! export default function LoginPage() { ... } ``` 4. EXACT FIX FOR EACH PATH: /api/webhook/stripe - Force dynamic rendering: ```typescript export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; // or 'force-static' if truly static export async function POST(req: Request) { const headersList = headers(); // Now called within request context // ... rest of webhook logic } ``` Or wrap in proper request handler: ```typescript export async function POST(req: Request) { const body = await req.json(); // Use req, not headers() at module level // ... } ``` /(auth)/login/page: ```typescript // Move auth calls inside component or server component export default async function LoginPage() { const session = await getSession(); // Now in request context return
...
; } // Or force dynamic export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; ``` 5. HOW USER COULD FIND THIS THEMSELVES: - Search: "Next.js Invariant Method expects to have requestAsyncStorage" - Check Next.js docs: "Dynamic vs Static Rendering" - Search Vercel deployment errors + "requestAsyncStorage" - Look at Next.js GitHub issues for this error - Check if using `headers()`, `cookies()`, `useSearchParams()` at module level - Review Next.js 13+ App Router docs on server components and request context - Key insight: Any code accessing request data must be inside a request context (route handler, server component body, not at module level)
